```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel scorecard-sidebar">
<details open>
<summary>Reading the scorecard</summary>
<p>The §4 table is the primary sanity check. Three conventions:</p>
<dl>
  <dt><code>z</code></dt>
  <dd><code>(MC − cf) / stderr</code>. <code>|z| ≤ 2</code> is healthy
  sampling noise; <code>|z| &gt; 3</code> points at a simulator bug.</dd>

  <dt><code>±CI<sub>95</sub></code></dt>
  <dd>95% half-width of the MC mean,
  <code>1.96·SD/√n</code>. Shrinks as <code>1/√n<sub>paths</sub></code>.</dd>

  <dt>Variance ratio</dt>
  <dd>Fee and back-to-back share <code>I<sub>T</sub></code>, so
  <code>Var[Π<sub>b2b</sub>]/Var[R<sub>fee</sub>] = (P/f)²</code> exactly.
  Algebraic, not statistical.</dd>

  <dt>VaR / CVaR</dt>
  <dd>Loss threshold / mean loss inside that 5% (or 1%) tail. On the
  fee book these read as "low end of revenue", not loss — revenue
  ≥ 0 by construction.</dd>

  <dt>Horizon total</dt>
  <dd>P&amp;L is total USD over <code>[0, T]</code>. Divide by
  <code>N = λT</code> for a per-tonne figure.</dd>

  <dt>§6 Merton row</dt>
  <dd>Expect <code>|z| ≲ 2</code> against the GBM closed form and
  <strong>SD (merton) &gt; SD (gbm-cf)</strong>. Flat SD means the
  overlay is inert; persistent large <code>|z|</code> means broken
  compensation.</dd>
</dl>
</details>
</aside>
```
